---
title: "Conservation Biology – GIS Tutorials"
subtitle: "Spatial analysis of biodiversity data with QGIS and R"
author: "Conservation Biology Course"
date: today
---

## Welcome

This course introduces the spatial tools used in modern conservation biology.
By the end of the two tutorials you will be able to:

- Load, reproject, and query spatial data in **QGIS**.
- Produce publication-quality maps and analyses in **R** using the `sf`, `terra`,
  `ggplot2`, and `tmap` packages.

## Course structure

| Tutorial | Topic | Software |
|----------|-------|----------|
| [QGIS Tutorial](qgis_tutorial.qmd) | Map layers, projections, spatial queries, map export | QGIS 3.x |
| [R Spatial Tutorial](r_spatial_tutorial.qmd) | Vector/raster analysis, species distribution mapping | R ≥ 4.2 |

## Data

Example data used throughout the tutorials live in the `data/` folder:

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `data/study_area.gpkg` | Study-area polygon (GeoPackage) |
| `data/species_observations.csv` | Georeferenced species occurrence records |
| `data/land_cover.tif` | Land-cover raster (GeoTIFF) |

> **Note:** The data files provided are synthetic example datasets created for
> teaching purposes. Replace them with your own field data when working through
> the exercises.

## Prerequisites

- Basic familiarity with GIS concepts (coordinate systems, vector vs. raster).
- R and RStudio (or any R IDE) installed.
- QGIS 3.28 LTR or newer installed.
- Quarto 1.4 or newer (only needed to re-render the tutorials).

## Getting started

Clone or download this repository, then open the project folder.  
Render all tutorials at once with:

```bash
quarto render
```
